title: Add fizzbuzz helper
body: Add a public fizzbuzz(n: int) -> list[str] function to math_utils.py. Return a list of length n where index i (1-based) is 'FizzBuzz' when i is divisible by both 3 and 5, 'Fizz' when divisible by 3 only, 'Buzz' when divisible by 5 only, and str(i) otherwise. Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering n=15 (full sequence), n=0 (empty list), and n=1 (single element '1').
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first for n=15, n=0, n=1, implement fizzbuzz in math_utils.py, run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: n=0 must return []; indices are 1-based not 0-based. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add max subarray helper
body: Add a public max_subarray(nums: list[int]) -> int function to math_utils.py. Return the sum of the contiguous subarray with the largest sum (Kadane's algorithm). Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering the classic example [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4] (expected 6), an all-negative input [-3,-1,-2] (expected -1), a single-element input [5] (expected 5), and an empty input [] (expected 0).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test for the four cases first, implement max_subarray using O(n) Kadane, run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: empty list returns 0; all-negative list returns the largest (least negative) element. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add two sum helper
body: Add a public two_sum(nums: list[int], target: int) -> list[int] function to math_utils.py. Return the indices of the two numbers in nums that add up to target. Assume exactly one solution exists and the same element cannot be used twice. Order of indices in the output does not matter. Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering [2,7,11,15] target 9 (expected [0,1]), [3,2,4] target 6 (expected [1,2]), and [3,3] target 6 (expected [0,1]).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test for the three cases first, implement two_sum using a one-pass hash map, run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: duplicate values like [3,3] must not reuse the same index. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add reverse string helper
body: Add a public reverse_string(s: list[str]) -> None function to math_utils.py that reverses the list in place using O(1) extra space (two pointers). Return None. Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering ['h','e','l','l','o'] (mutated to ['o','l','l','e','h']), ['a'] (mutated to ['a']), [] (stays []), and ['a','b'] (mutated to ['b','a']).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first, implement reverse_string with left/right pointer swap in place, run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: empty list and single-element list are no-ops; do not allocate a new list. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add palindrome number helper
body: Add a public is_palindrome(x: int) -> bool function to math_utils.py. Return True if x reads the same forwards and backwards as an integer, False otherwise. Negative numbers are not palindromes. The function must not convert the integer to a string. Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering 121 (True), -121 (False), 10 (False), 0 (True), and 12321 (True).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first, implement is_palindrome by reversing half the number arithmetically, run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: negative numbers always False; numbers ending in 0 (except 0 itself) are False. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add valid anagram helper
body: Add a public is_anagram(s: str, t: str) -> bool function to math_utils.py. Return True if t is an anagram of s, False otherwise. Assume inputs contain only lowercase English letters. Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering ('anagram','nagaram') (True), ('rat','car') (False), ('a','a') (True), ('a','b') (False), and ('ab','ba') (True).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first, implement is_anagram with a fixed-size 26-bucket count array, run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: different lengths are immediately False; same length with different letter counts is False. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add roman to integer helper
body: Add a public roman_to_int(s: str) -> int function to math_utils.py. Convert a Roman numeral string (symbols I,V,X,L,C,D,M with values 1,5,10,50,100,500,1000) to its integer value. When a smaller symbol appears before a larger one, subtract it (e.g. IV=4, IX=9, XL=40, XC=90, CD=400, CM=900). Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering 'III' (3), 'LVIII' (58), 'MCMXCIV' (1994), 'IV' (4), and 'XLII' (42).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first, implement roman_to_int with a single left-to-right pass (add when current >= previous, otherwise subtract previous), run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: subtractive pairs are the only ones to subtract; never subtract two in a row. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add contains duplicate helper
body: Add a public contains_duplicate(nums: list[int]) -> bool function to math_utils.py. Return True if any value appears at least twice in the list, False if all elements are distinct. Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering [1,2,3,1] (True), [1,2,3,4] (False), [1,1,1,1] (True), [] (False), and [1] (False).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first, implement contains_duplicate with a single set pass, run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: empty list and single-element list always return False. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add fibonacci helper
body: Add a public fib(n: int) -> int function to math_utils.py. Return the n-th Fibonacci number where fib(0)=0 and fib(1)=1, so the sequence is 0,1,1,2,3,5,8,13,21,... Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering n=0 (0), n=1 (1), n=2 (1), n=5 (5), n=10 (55), and n=20 (6765).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first, implement fib iteratively with O(n) time and O(1) space (no recursion, no memoization needed), run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: n=0 returns 0; n=1 returns 1; do not use recursion to avoid stack issues for large n. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.

title: Add binary search helper
body: Add a public binary_search(nums: list[int], target: int) -> int function to math_utils.py. Return the index of target in the ascending-sorted list nums, or -1 if not found. Add pytest tests in tests/test_math_utils.py covering ([-1,0,3,5,9,12], 9) (4), ([-1,0,3,5,9,12], 2) (-1), ([5], 5) (0), ([], 1) (-1), and ([1,2,3,4,5], 1) (0).
explorer_report: Affected files are math_utils.py and tests/test_math_utils.py. Add a failing parametrize test first, implement binary_search iteratively with standard left/right bounds (left <= right, mid = (left+right)//2), run `uv run pytest`, then commit once. Edge cases: empty list returns -1; target equal to a boundary element must be found. Test surface: pytest. Risks: do not touch unrelated files.
